title: U.S. Election Records by PolitiFinder
subtitle: Biggest landslides, closest races, most votes, and more
description:
By PolitiFinder (https://politifinder.com): the most striking records in U.S. election history across President, U.S. Senate, U.S. House, and Governor.

Includes ~12 record categories: biggest presidential landslides, closest presidential elections, most popular votes in one election, highest popular-vote percentage, most electoral votes, presidents who won while losing the popular vote, closest U.S. Senate / House / Governor races, strongest third-party and independent runs, youngest and oldest U.S. senators, and career popular-vote leaders (named-candidate basis).

One row per record (115 total). Names and public PolitiFinder links only - no underlying race-by-race data. Career totals count a person only as the named candidate; running-mate (vice-presidential) ticket votes are excluded.

Provenance: derived from PolitiFinder's compilation of official U.S. election results (FEC, the Clerk of the U.S. House, the MIT Election Data and Science Lab, the Constituency-Level Elections Archive, official state canvasses, ICPSR, and A New Nation Votes). Every record reconciles to its page at https://politifinder.com/rankings. Updated after each general election. Licensed CC BY 4.0.

resources.description: 115 records across ~12 categories (one row per record entry).

fields.name: record
fields.description: The record category, e.g. 'Biggest presidential landslides'.

fields.name: subject
fields.description: Candidate name or election the record is about (display text).

fields.name: detail
fields.description: Short context, e.g. 'def. James M. Cox' or 'Minnesota - def. Norm Coleman'.

fields.name: unit
fields.description: Unit of the value: points, votes, %, electoral votes, or years.

fields.name: office
fields.description: President, U.S. Senate, U.S. House, or Governor.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Public PolitiFinder page for that record or candidate.

resources.description: JSON copy of the same 115 records.
